verdict.answer_first: B opens cleaner: "It's a data-leak bug waiting to happen"; A also leads well with "cross-request data leak waiting to happen," but adds "Short version" padding.
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly more prescriptive: "Don't hold request state in module scope. Pass it explicitly" before naming AsyncLocalStorage as fallback; B gives the same fix but less forcefully.
verdict.fabrication: B overstates with "it passes every local test"; A's "tests usually miss it" is also not proven, but less absolute.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to implement because it gives both the direct code fix `render(req.user)` and a concrete AsyncLocalStorage snippet.
